title: Cost Management
lede: See your prepaid balance, understand how each call and message is priced, top up funds, and review every charge on the Billing page.

steps.title: Open the Billing page
steps.content:
1. In the left menu, under Account, open Billing (at /billing).
  2. The Prepaid Balance card shows your current balance — the funds available to pay for calls and messages. Usage is drawn down from this balance as it happens.

images.caption: The Billing page shows your prepaid balance, add-funds panel, and transaction tabs
The Billing page shows your prepaid balance, add-funds panel, and transaction tabs

steps.title: Understand how a call is priced
steps.content:
Every call is priced per usage, not as a flat fee. iMash adds up the cost of each part ("leg") of the call — the phone minutes, the AI language model, the voice (text-to-speech), and the transcription (speech-to-text) — and stores the total on the call. Messages (SMS/WhatsApp) are priced per message.

  • Per-minute charges apply to voice calls and talk time.
  • Per-message charges apply to each SMS or WhatsApp message.

You can see the spend those charges produce as Total spent and Avg cost / call on the Overview page, and per campaign in Campaign Reports.

steps.title: Top up your balance
steps.content:
1. In the Add Funds panel, enter the amount you want to add.
  2. Choose a payment method — a saved card or a bank account (ACH).
  3. Note the processing note: bank (ACH) payments have no fee and credit the full amount, while card payments carry a small processing fee, so the amount credited to your balance is slightly less than the amount charged.
  4. Click Add Funds to complete the top-up. Your balance updates once the payment confirms.

steps.title: Review your charges
steps.content:
• Open the Ledger tab to see every transaction — top-ups and usage charges — with running balance.
  • Open the Phone Number Order tab to review your monthly phone-number subscriptions.
  • For a call-by-call breakdown of spend, use the Overview page filters or Campaign Reports.

support.content:
